title: Chargers troll Patriots over Mike Vrabel, Dianna Russini, and more in schedule release video
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Chargers' schedule release video featured digs at Patriots' coach Mike Vrabel and former reporter Dianna Russini.
The Halo-themed video pokes fun at the Patriots' Super Bowl loss and includes a Route 1 T-Rex tribute.
The Chargers acknowledged their 2025 playoff loss to the Patriots with a reference code during the video.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz on trending stories in Greater Boston as of May 2026.
The quiz is compiled by Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor.
Peter Chianca has been with Boston.com since 2019 and focuses on local news coverage.

title: Review and setlist: Courtney Barnett shows that being a ‘creature of habit’ isn’t always a bad thing
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Courtney Barnett performed at Roadrunner, Boston, promoting her fourth album 'Creature of Habit' on May 13, 2026.
Barnett's setlist included songs from her new album and hits like 'Avant Gardener' and 'Depreston'.
Brooklyn-based band Momma and musician Truman Sinclair opened for Barnett during her Boston concert.

title: MassGOP-backed lieutenant governor candidate fails to collect enough signatures to make ballot
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: GOP lieutenant governor candidate Anne Brensley failed to collect 10,000 signatures required to get on the ballot.
Brensley's campaign blamed a hired signature gatherer, Joe Bronske, amid concerns of fraudulent signatures.
Secretary of the Commonwealth cannot extend the deadline; Brensley considers running as a write-in candidate.

title: 7 takeaways from Patriots’ 2026 schedule
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots' 2026 schedule includes eight games against 2025 playoff teams and features six nationally televised matchups.
New England will face the Seahawks in a Super Bowl LX rematch on September 9, marking the first ever Wednesday game for the team.
Patriots will travel 27,980 miles in 2026, including a game in Munich, Germany against the Detroit Lions on November 15.

title: Here’s your chance to design a new Boston Public Library card
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston Public Library is hosting a library card design contest from May 15 to June 15.
Winners from three age categories will have their artwork featured on new library cards starting in September.
Submissions should be original, high-resolution designs reflecting library themes, with AI-generated content prohibited.

title: ‘This was my baby’: Blaze destroys popular Belmont ice cream shop, causes floor to collapse
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A three-alarm fire early Thursday destroyed Moozy's Ice Cream Emporium in Belmont, causing the floor to collapse.
A ruptured natural gas line caused a second fire; neighboring businesses suffered smoke and water damage.
The cause of the fire at Moozy's is under investigation, with hopes to rebuild despite extensive damage.

title: Patriots officially unveil 2026 schedule that begins with Super Bowl rematch
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots open the 2026 NFL season with a Super Bowl rematch against the Seahawks on September 9 in Seattle.
The Patriots will play an international game against the Detroit Lions in Munich, Germany on November 15.
New England's 2026 schedule features six nationally televised matchups, including games on Thursday, Sunday, and Monday nights.

title: Did my vehicle miss out on an important safety recall?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2009 VW Jetta owner missed a 2017 safety recall involving ABS module issues; no notification was received.
Recalls often apply to specific production runs; affected vehicle owners can seek reimbursement if repairs match recall conditions.
To pursue reimbursement, owners should contact Volkswagen customer service and the NHTSA, and send repair documentation by letter.

title: Bruins assistant GM leaving organization to ‘pursue other opportunities’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jamie Langenbrunner leaves Boston Bruins after 11 years to explore NHL opportunities.
Langenbrunner was responsible for player personnel as assistant GM since 2022.
Potential replacements for Langenbrunner include Adam McQuaid and Zdeno Chara in Bruins' front office.
